function_explanation: Computes the gradient of the data fidelity term for a multi-coil MRI reconstruction problem.
function_docstring:
Compute the gradient of the data fidelity term using the forward operator for multi-coil MRI.

Args:
    x (torch.Tensor): A tensor of shape (B, C, H, W) representing the input image, where B is the batch size, C is the number of channels, and H, W are the height and width of the image.
    observation (torch.Tensor): A tensor of shape (B, C, H, W) representing the observed k-space data.
    maps (torch.Tensor): A tensor of shape (B, C, H, W) representing the coil sensitivity maps.
    mask (torch.Tensor): A tensor of shape (B, C, H, W) representing the undersampling mask applied in k-space.

Returns:
    torch.Tensor: A tensor of shape (B, C, H, W) representing the gradient of the data fidelity term with respect to the input image.
usage_example:
import torch

# Example tensors
x = torch.randn((B, C, H, W), dtype=torch.float64)
observation = torch.randn((B, C, H, W), dtype=torch.float64)
maps = torch.randn((B, C, H, W), dtype=torch.float64)
mask = torch.ones((B, C, H, W), dtype=torch.float64)

# Compute gradient
gradient = compute_gradient(x, observation, maps, mask)
print(gradient)
